value.found_quote: Sandboxing provides kernel-level isolation for terminal commands executed by the Agent. When enabled, commands run in a restricted environment with limited file system and network access, protecting your system from unintended modifications.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes "Terminal Sandboxing" as a feature of the Antigravity IDE, confirming kernel-level isolation for terminal commands executed by the Agent in a restricted environment. It further details that the feature can be enabled/disabled via user settings (permission grants), leverages OS-level mechanisms (macOS Seatbelt/sandbox-exec and Linux nsjail), and controls file system and network access — all consistent with the claim. The feature is described as available (though disabled by default), indicating an active lifecycle status.
